concept: Maintain / Keep in Condition / Sustain Functioning
description: To keep something in good working condition through ongoing attention and care. 'Thiranu' names the practice of upkeep—the regular attention that keeps tools, relationships, systems, and bodies functioning well over time.
sound_symbolism: The word sustains through attention. 'Thi' begins with careful dental awareness, the precise attention that maintenance demands. 'Ra' continues with steady rhythm. 'Nu' grounds with lasting presence. To maintain as ongoing, mindful care.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia lo thena thiranu.' (I maintain things.) Can specify what: 'mia lo lumani nolami thiranu.' (I maintain family bonds.) 'mia thepalu thiranu.' (I maintain the garden.) Related to nelore (preserve) but emphasizes active upkeep rather than protection from change.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Maintenance culture values repair and upkeep over replacement—caring for what we have rather than discarding and replacing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional crafts require ongoing maintenance—tools, buildings, and textiles last generations through regular care.
